evidence.snippet: NIT designs and manufactures SWIR InGaAs sensors & cameras
Ideal for **ISR** and **semiconductor inspection** **applications**.

**New Imaging Technologies (NIT)**, part of [LYNRED](https://www.lynred.com/), a leading designer and manufacturer of **high-performance SWIR InGaAs sensors and cameras**, supports this application with a complete range of **lightweight**, **high-resolution SWIR imaging solutions optimized for UAV integration.**

items.notes: Designs and manufactures SWIR InGaAs cameras and sensors explicitly used for ISR, security/surveillance, airborne fire-fighting, and UAV integration. CMOS cameras (MC1003, MN1003, MC1105F) are additional EO imaging products.

evidence.snippet:
**New Imaging Technologies (NIT)**
1 impasse de la Noisette
91370 Verrières le Buisson – France
Tel: +33 1 64 47 88 58
*   Ideal for **OEM drone payload developers**

With its complete portfolio of SWIR sensors, modules, and cameras, NIT supports UAV manufacturers, system integrators, and solar inspection specialists in deploying reliable, high-performance aerial inspection solutions.

*   **Naked sensor modules** and **board-level SWIR cameras**
*   Lightweight and compact form factors
*   Easy integration into custom gimbals or payloads
*   Optimized for **embedded vision and aerial platforms**

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: NIT sells EO/IR cameras and bare InGaAs sensors to UAV manufacturers and system integrators for integration into drone payloads. Products are components/modules that go into customer-built payload pods rather than complete payload subsystems with housings/gimbals.

items.name: WiDy SenS 640
items.product_type: HDR SWIR InGaAs camera with SDI output

evidence.snippet:
**WiDy SenS 640 with SDI output**
---------------------------------
The **WiDy SenS 640H-STE** provides straightforward **digital video SDI output** interfacing with the most current electronic **video platforms**.
Ideal for **airborne fire fighting**, **search and rescue**, and **agricultural mapping**.

**WiDy SenS 320**
-----------------
*   QVGA SWIR camera
*   Unique dual-response mode **HDR & High sensitivity,**
*   delivering **up to 10kHz (ROI), 1kHz full frame**
*   [WiDy SWIR 640](https://new-imaging-technologies.com/product/widy-swir-640/)

items.name: WiDy NaNo 640
items.product_type: Compact SWIR InGaAs camera (board-level UAV module)

evidence.snippet: *   [WiDy NaNo 640](https://new-imaging-technologies.com/product/widy-nano-640/)

-----------------
High-performance SWIR camera with **low noise**, **high sensitivity**, and **long exposure time** with simple **air cooling**.
Best performance and lowest price of its class for **scientific** and **medical applications**.

**LiSaSWIR 2048 v2**
--------------------
**Line-scan** array camera with **2048 pixels** for industrial inspection.
**High-speed & High-performance**

ndaa.notes: NIT is a French company (LYNRED subsidiary). No NDAA Sec. 848/889 claim in corpus. Footer shows 'Made in France' logo.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS or Blue sUAS mention in the corpus.

readiness.notes: Multiple catalog SKUs with detailed specs listed. News confirms new models 'available for order' and 'ready for commercial deployment'.

unresolved_questions: NDAA Sec. 848/889 compliance status not disclosed on site
Individual SWIR camera product pages were skipped — detailed drone-specific specs not fully confirmed

fetch_requests.reason: Would confirm corporate history, LYNRED parent relationship, and any US operations or compliance claims relevant to NDAA and drone_supply_chain_role classification.

WiDy NaNo 640 is specifically described for tight UAV integrations; product page would confirm SWaP specs and whether it ships as a complete payload module or bare board, clarifying component_supplier vs subsystem_supplier role.

tagline: French SWIR InGaAs and HDR CMOS camera manufacturer producing airborne-ready payloads (SenS 1920 Full HD, WiDy series VGA–QVGA, line-scan LiSaSWIR 2048) in production. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Public evidence emphasizes industrial and scientific applications; UAV/ISR positioning cited on homepage but limited open contract or program data; no U.S. supply-chain footprint evident.
